title: Stop Making Models Bigger, Make Them Behave — Kobie Crawdord, Snorkel
summary: Kobie Crawford from Snorkel demonstrates that a 4B model fine-tuned with reinforcement learning to use tools like `get_table_name` outperformed Qwen 3 235B on database query tasks, achieving correct answers through tool discipline and self-correction. The approach achieved 26.6% on FinQA with single-table transfer learning and cost under 500 dollars to train.
writeup: Kobie Crawford of Snorkel argues that behavior, not parameter count, drives performance on database query tasks. The central result: a 4B model fine-tuned with reinforcement learning to call tools like get_table_name outperformed Qwen 3 235B, reaching correct answers through tool discipline and self-correction. The approach scored 26.6% on FinQA using single-table transfer learning and cost under 500 dollars to train, showing that a small model with structured tool use can beat a far larger one at a fraction of the spend.
